decisions.summary: The actual bug fix must be applied to eltmon-stream/tts_daemon.py (external to this workspace). The daemon's play_audio() was using per-utterance aplay with a fixed 400ms silence prepend, which is insufficient for PipeWire + USB DAC resume (~500ms). The fix replaces aplay with a persistent pw-play stdin stream, keeps it open for 10s after the last utterance, and adapts silence prepend based on sink state (600ms for SUSPENDED, 50ms for RUNNING).

Since eltmon-stream has no GitHub remote and the issue is tracked in panopticon-cli, the fix was committed locally in eltmon-stream and a documentation update was committed in panopticon-cli (skills/pan-tts/SKILL.md) to reflect the correct split architecture and document the PipeWire suspend mitigation.

hazards.summary: The workspace was created on panopticon-cli but the actual code to fix is in eltmon-stream (a separate local git repo with no remote). The normal pan done / PR flow targets panopticon-cli.
hazards.mitigation: Committed the daemon fix in eltmon-stream locally, committed a documentation update in panopticon-cli to reflect the architecture, and pushed the panopticon-cli branch for PR creation.

resumePoint.description: Fix committed in eltmon-stream and documentation updated in panopticon-cli. Remaining step: run pan done PAN-1028 to signal completion and trigger the review pipeline.

sessionHistory.note: Found workspace with no PAN-1028 beads and a deleted stale PAN-986 continue.json. Identified the issue as TTS audio clipping in eltmon-stream/tts_daemon.py. Implemented persistent pw-play stream with adaptive silence prepend (600ms cold start, 50ms warm). Committed fix in eltmon-stream and updated panopticon-cli skill docs.
